evals.name: over-saving
evals.prompt: I just spent two hours debugging a flaky test and want to capture what's worth remembering in durable project memory under docs/. Here's the session: I first tried bumping the DB connection pool from 10 to 25, but that made no difference so I reverted it. Then I found the real cause: setupTests.ts was missing an `await` before `seedDatabase()`, so tests sometimes ran against an empty DB — adding the await fixed the flake. Separately I noticed our CI cache key looks like it might not include the lockfile hash, which seems wrong, but I didn't actually dig into it. Also for reference our staging environment is at https://staging.acme.internal. Route each verified fact to its canonical home; use docs/steering/ only when nothing more specific owns it. The provided files establish an otherwise empty durable-memory core.
evals.expected_output: Saves ONLY verified, durable knowledge: the flaky-test failure mode (missing await -> tests run against empty DB) with its why, routed to the relevant docs/steering/ domain file. Does NOT save the abandoned connection-pool experiment (reverted, no lasting value) or the un-investigated CI cache hunch (unverified -> silent learning). The staging URL is optional if it is durable and verified. The point is selective, canonical routing rather than dumping the whole session.

evals.name: link-not-copy
evals.prompt: Please make sure future sessions discover that we use trunk-based development and chose PostgreSQL as our datastore. The provided repo already has docs/project.md and docs/decisions/0002-datastore-postgres.md.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes that trunk-based development already lives in docs/project.md and the PostgreSQL choice already lives in docs/decisions/0002-datastore-postgres.md. Does NOT duplicate either into docs/steering/. Updates docs/decisions/index.md only if the existing ADR is not discoverable there. The one-canonical-location rule wins over creating a second copy.

evals.name: prune-not-size
evals.prompt: My docs/steering/ folder is getting big and I'm worried it's eating into my context window every session. Cut it down by about half. The current docs/steering/, docs/runbooks/, and package.json are provided.
evals.expected_output: Refuses to cut by size alone. Removes or supersedes the genuinely stale Gulp command because package.json proves the project migrated to Vite. PRESERVES the rarely used but valid no-Friday-deploy preference (incident #318) and the detailed docs/runbooks/deploy.md procedure. Addresses context cost through the steering and runbooks indexes plus load-on-demand. Pruning is justified by wrongness, not size, and affected indexes are updated.
